[chat-message-theming] [P3] Migrate ToolCallCard to semantic status tokens
tasks.description:
Replace hardcoded Tailwind color classes (`text-blue-500`, `text-green-500`, `text-red-500`) in `ToolCallCard.tsx` with semantic status token classes from `toolStatus()` TV variant.

## Files Modified
- `apps/client/src/layers/features/chat/ui/ToolCallCard.tsx`

## Implementation Steps

### Step 1: Add imports

Add to the top of `ToolCallCard.tsx`:

```typescript
import { cn } from '@/layers/shared/lib';
import { toolStatus } from './message/message-variants';
```

Note: `cn` may already be imported; if not, add it.

### Step 2: Replace statusIcon color classes

Find the `statusIcon` object (currently around line 15-20) and replace:

```typescript
// Before:
const statusIcon = {
  pending: <Loader2 className="size-(--size-icon-xs) animate-spin" />,
  running: <Loader2 className="size-(--size-icon-xs) animate-spin text-blue-500" />,
  complete: <Check className="size-(--size-icon-xs) text-green-500" />,
  error: <X className="size-(--size-icon-xs) text-red-500" />,
}[toolCall.status];

// After:
const statusIcon = {
  pending: <Loader2 className={cn('size-(--size-icon-xs) animate-spin', toolStatus({ status: 'pending' }))} />,
  running: <Loader2 className={cn('size-(--size-icon-xs) animate-spin', toolStatus({ status: 'running' }))} />,
  complete: <Check className={cn('size-(--size-icon-xs)', toolStatus({ status: 'complete' }))} />,
  error: <X className={cn('size-(--size-icon-xs)', toolStatus({ status: 'error' }))} />,
}[toolCall.status];
```

The `toolStatus()` TV variant returns semantic class strings:
- `pending` -> `text-status-pending`
- `running` -> `text-status-info`
- `complete` -> `text-status-success`
- `error` -> `text-status-error`

These reference the CSS custom properties defined in task 1.1, which resolve to the same visual colors as the hardcoded values they replace.

## Verification
- Run `pnpm test -- --run` and check that `ToolCallCard.test.tsx` tests pass
- Note: Existing tests that assert on specific class names like `text-blue-500` will need updating in task 4.1
- Visually verify ToolCallCard status icons show the same colors as before

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] No hardcoded color classes (`text-blue-500`, `text-green-500`, `text-red-500`) remain in ToolCallCard.tsx
- [ ] All status icon colors use `toolStatus()` TV variant
- [ ] `cn()` is used to merge the TV output with the base icon classes
- [ ] Visual appearance is identical to before (same hues for each status)
- [ ] `pnpm typecheck` passes
tasks.activeForm: Migrating ToolCallCard to semantic status tokens

tasks.subject: [chat-message-theming] [P3] Migrate ToolApproval to semantic tokens and ref-as-prop
tasks.description:
Replace hardcoded Tailwind color classes in `ToolApproval.tsx` with semantic status token classes from `approvalState()` TV variant. Also migrate from `forwardRef` to React 19 ref-as-prop pattern.

## Files Modified
- `apps/client/src/layers/features/chat/ui/ToolApproval.tsx`

## Implementation Steps

### Step 1: Add import for approvalState

Add to imports:

```typescript
import { approvalState } from './message/message-variants';
```

### Step 2: Migrate from forwardRef to ref-as-prop

Change the component signature from:

```typescript
export const ToolApproval = forwardRef<ToolApprovalHandle, ToolApprovalProps>(function ToolApproval(
  { sessionId, toolCallId, toolName, input, isActive = false, onDecided },
  ref
) {
```

To:

```typescript
export function ToolApproval({
  sessionId,
  toolCallId,
  toolName,
  input,
  isActive = false,
  onDecided,
  ref,
}: ToolApprovalProps & { ref?: React.Ref<ToolApprovalHandle> }) {
```

Remove the `forwardRef` import from the import list (keep `useImperativeHandle`). The `useImperativeHandle(ref, ...)` call inside the component stays the same.

### Step 3: Replace decided state styling

Find the decided state rendering (the early return when `decided` is not null). Replace hardcoded colors:

```typescript
// Before:
decided === 'approved'
  ? 'border-emerald-500/20 bg-emerald-500/10 text-emerald-600 dark:text-emerald-400'
  : 'border-red-500/20 bg-red-500/10 text-red-600 dark:text-red-400'

// After:
approvalState({ state: decided })
```

The full decided block becomes:
```typescript
<div className={cn(
  'my-1 rounded border px-3 py-2 text-sm transition-colors duration-200',
  approvalState({ state: decided })
)} />
```

### Step 4: Replace pending state styling

Find the pending (undecided) state container. Replace hardcoded amber colors:

```typescript
// Before:
'border-amber-500/20 bg-amber-500/10'
isActive && 'ring-2 ring-amber-500/30'

// After:
cn(
  'my-1 rounded border p-3 text-sm transition-all duration-200',
  approvalState({ state: 'pending' }),
  isActive && 'ring-2 ring-status-warning/30'
)
```

### Step 5: Replace button colors

Find the approve and deny buttons. Replace:

```typescript
// Approve button:
// Before: 'bg-emerald-600 hover:bg-emerald-700'
// After: 'bg-status-success hover:bg-status-success/90'

// Deny button:
// Before: 'bg-red-600 hover:bg-red-700'
// After: 'bg-status-error hover:bg-status-error/90'
```

## Color Mapping Reference

| Before (hardcoded) | After (semantic) |
|---|---|
| `border-emerald-500/20` | `border-status-success-border` (via `approvalState({ state: 'approved' })`) |
| `bg-emerald-500/10` | `bg-status-success-bg` (via `approvalState({ state: 'approved' })`) |
| `text-emerald-600 dark:text-emerald-400` | `text-status-success-fg` (via `approvalState({ state: 'approved' })`) |
| `border-red-500/20` | `border-status-error-border` (via `approvalState({ state: 'denied' })`) |
| `bg-red-500/10` | `bg-status-error-bg` (via `approvalState({ state: 'denied' })`) |
| `text-red-600 dark:text-red-400` | `text-status-error-fg` (via `approvalState({ state: 'denied' })`) |
| `border-amber-500/20` | `border-status-warning-border` (via `approvalState({ state: 'pending' })`) |
| `bg-amber-500/10` | `bg-status-warning-bg` (via `approvalState({ state: 'pending' })`) |
| `ring-amber-500/30` | `ring-status-warning/30` |
| `bg-emerald-600 hover:bg-emerald-700` | `bg-status-success hover:bg-status-success/90` |
| `bg-red-600 hover:bg-red-700` | `bg-status-error hover:bg-status-error/90` |

## Verification
- Run `pnpm typecheck` to confirm the ref-as-prop migration compiles
- Visually verify ToolApproval renders the same colors in all 3 states (pending, approved, denied)
- Note: Test assertions for specific class names will need updating in task 4.1

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] No `forwardRef` usage remains in ToolApproval.tsx
- [ ] Component uses ref-as-prop pattern (`ref` in the props destructuring)
- [ ] No hardcoded color classes (`emerald-*`, `red-*`, `amber-*`) remain
- [ ] All state styling uses `approvalState()` TV variant
- [ ] Button colors use `bg-status-success` and `bg-status-error` tokens
- [ ] Active ring uses `ring-status-warning/30`
- [ ] Visual appearance is identical to before
- [ ] `pnpm typecheck` passes
tasks.activeForm: Migrating ToolApproval to semantic tokens and ref-as-prop

tasks.subject: [chat-message-theming] [P5] Update design-system and styling docs for status tokens and TV
tasks.description:
Update the internal documentation to reflect the new semantic token system and tailwind-variants usage.

## Files Modified
- `contributing/design-system.md`
- `contributing/styling-theming.md`
- `AGENTS.md`

## Implementation Steps

### Step 1: Update contributing/design-system.md

Add a new section titled "Status Tokens" after the existing color palette section. Document:

- The 5 status categories: success, error, warning, info, pending
- Each category's 4 tokens: base, -bg, -border, -fg
- Usage examples: `text-status-success`, `bg-status-error-bg`, `border-status-warning-border`
- The purpose: replacing hardcoded emerald/red/blue/amber/green color classes throughout the codebase
- Light vs dark mode values are defined in `:root` and `.dark` blocks in `index.css`

Also add a "Message Tokens" subsection documenting:
- `--msg-*` tokens for typography, spacing, shape, motion, interactive states, and elevation
- Their purpose: enabling density settings and theme customization without touching component code

Update the Message component section (if it exists) to reference TV variants instead of inline cn() conditionals.

### Step 2: Update contributing/styling-theming.md

Add a section titled "tailwind-variants (TV)" that documents:

- When to use TV vs CVA:
  - **TV**: Multi-slot components with role/state variants (message items, cards with header/body/footer slots)
  - **CVA**: Single-element primitives (buttons, badges, inputs) — used by shadcn
- Both output class strings and coexist in the same codebase
- TV is imported from `tailwind-variants`, CVA from `class-variance-authority`
- Example of the `messageItem` TV definition showing slots and variants
- How to consume TV: `const styles = messageItem({ role, position }); <div className={styles.root()} />`

### Step 3: Update AGENTS.md

In the client FSD layer table (the `features/chat/` row), add mention of the `ui/message/` sub-module:

```
| `features/chat/`         | ChatPanel, MessageList, MessageItem, message/ sub-module (MessageContext, UserMessageContent, AssistantMessageContent, message-variants), ToolCallCard, useChatSession  | Chat interface              |
```

Also add `tailwind-variants` to the Technical Dependencies section if it exists, noting it coexists with CVA.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `contributing/design-system.md` has a Status Tokens section with all 5 categories documented
- [ ] `contributing/design-system.md` has a Message Tokens section
- [ ] `contributing/styling-theming.md` has a TV section explaining TV vs CVA decision criteria
- [ ] `AGENTS.md` FSD layer table mentions the `message/` sub-module
- [ ] No generated/placeholder content — all documentation reflects the actual implementation
tasks.activeForm: Updating documentation for status tokens and TV patterns
